versions.author: fable
changes: DeepSeek system prompt: when shown a mechanically translated HIP draft, plan only the wavefront64 semantics delta as a terse checklist
DeepSeek plan prompt: hipified_source switches it to a delta prompt with no CUDA original embedded (15555 -> 4965 chars on nvidia_shfl_scan.cu), capped at PLAN_DELTA_MAX_TOKENS=640
GLM error-analyst prompt: self_contained flag stops it reporting a truncated-away main() as a defect, and pins the fix to 'restore main() from the original source'
main() preservation: _extract_main / _ensure_main_preserved / _postprocess_port reattach a dropped driver before the first compile, with no LLM call
Linker-only short-circuit: _is_linker_only skips the GLM analyst and both DeepSeek re-plans when every diagnostic is a link failure
Budget-aware refine dispatch: a refine (and its retry) is skipped unless the clock holds the call plus COMPILE_RESERVE_SECONDS

versions.author: fable
changes: Kimi code prompt: edit a mechanically hipified draft instead of re-porting from scratch
Kimi code prompt: checklist narrowed from 9 items to the 4 wavefront64 semantics a regex cannot do
Kimi refine prompt: state that the mechanical pass is applied; name cuda* symbols that crept back
Output-token budget sized to the kernel via _compute_adaptive_max_tokens instead of a flat 16384

versions.author: fable
changes: Initial versioned system prompts for DeepSeek (planner), Kimi (coder), GLM (evaluator)
Moved the GLM error-analyst role from an inline call-site string into SYSTEM_PROMPTS
Added the two-layer SIGSEGV constraint to the Kimi refine prompt (frozen Layer 1 base)
Refine prompt now reports the real stagnation threshold instead of a hardcoded 3
